## Chronology Spine

## Core Truth Set
- (5/5) Meaning extraction begins with listening (tone, spacing, breath) prior to visual confirmation. | Quote: "She listened first. Before she looked. ... Tone. Spacing. Breath. That was where meaning lived."
- (5/5) Fire configuration is intentionally low-signature (coal-heavy, low-flame) to minimize distant visibility while preserving close-range utility. | Quote: "The fire was not meant to be seen from far away ... more coal than flame, more glow than brightness."
- (5/5) Observer intelligence depends on deliberate body-position and silhouette control to gather signal without drawing notice. | Quote: "She had chosen this place carefully ... not forward ... forward would break her outline."
- (5/5) Authority is legible before speech through interruption suppression and group behavioral accommodation. | Quote: "The woman did not speak immediately ... she controlled the space ... no one interrupted her."
- (4/5) Under constrained light, movement-pattern reading substitutes for facial interpretation and remains decision-relevant. | Quote: "Faces were harder ... But she did not need faces. She watched movement."

## Setting Anchors
- Low-Signature Forest Clearing [FIELD]: Depressed fire pit council edge where acoustic containment, light discipline, and silhouette management shape conflict observation.

## Continuity Constraints
- Signature Management Protocol: Operational stealth constraint.
- Edge Observer Intelligence Discipline: Observer tradecraft constraint.
- Preverbal Power Recognition: Authority-legibility constraint.

## Open Research Gaps
- What material and procedural standards define low-signature council setup across terrain types? (priority 2): Needed to keep stealth gathering scenes operationally consistent.
- When is edge observation tolerated versus sanctioned in sensitive gatherings? (priority 2): Needed to govern watcher participation realism and risk consequences.
- Which nonverbal cues most reliably indicate authority before speech in this system? (priority 2): Needed for consistent power-legibility writing under low-visibility conditions.
